channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: Unannounced 32TB Seagate hard drive surfaces in Japan for photoshoot
item.description: Seagate’s unannounced 32TB IronWolf Pro hard drive surfaced in Japanese retail, marking a quiet shift of extreme capacity toward consumers.
item.dc:creator: Efosa Udinmwen, TechRadar

item.title: AI may not be the federal buzzword for 2026
item.description: Federal News Network asked federal acquisition and IT experts what initiatives they are paying close attention to in the new year. The post AI may not be the federal buzzword for 2026 first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Jason Miller, Federal News Network

item.title: European banks may lay off 200,000 due to AI
item.description: Over 200,000 banking jobs in Europe could disappear by 2030 as banks invest more in AI and close physical branches according to a new analysis by Morgan Stanley reported by the Financial Times. This is equivalent to around 10% of the workforce at 35 major banks.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Deepseek says new method can train AI more efficiently and cheaply
item.description: Chinese AI company Deepseek has unveiled a new training method, Manifold-Constrained Hyper-Connections (mHC), which will make it possible to train large language models more efficiently and at lower cost, reports the South China Morning Post.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Las mejores entrevistas a líderes de TI en CIO ESPAÑA en 2025
item.description: Sonia Segade, directora de Transformación Digital y Tecnología de Renfe La apuesta por la inteligencia artificial, la hiperautomatización, el análisis de datos, la ciberseguridad y la convergencia entre las tecnologías de información y de operación son algunas de las palancas de futuro de Renfe, seg
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: FAA ramps up billions in spending as ‘down payment’ for air traffic overhaul
item.description: The agency expects to spend more than $6 billion this year to lay the groundwork for a complete overhaul of the rapidly aging air traffic control system. The post FAA ramps up billions in spending as ‘down payment’ for air traffic overhaul first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Jared Serbu, Federal News Network

item.title: 10 simple cybersecurity resolutions for a safer 2026
item.description: Learn how to act against cybercriminals in 2026 with essential security steps like two-factor authentication, software updates and credit freezing.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: How to manually update Microsoft Defender
item.description: Microsoft Defender is the built-in anti-malware package that’s included with modern Windows operating systems.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: AI agents 2025 recap: What happened and what to expect next year
item.description: In artificial intelligence, 2025 marked a decisive shift. Systems once confined to research labs and prototypes began to appear as everyday tools. At the center of this transition was the rise of AI agents—AI systems that can use other software tools and act on their own.
item.dc:creator: The Conversation, Fast Company

item.title: Meta buys startup Manus in latest move to advance its artificial intelligence efforts
item.description: Meta is buying artificial intelligence startup Manus, as the owner of Facebook and Instagram continues a push to amp-up AI offerings across its platforms.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Nvidia licenses Groq’s inferencing chip tech and hires its leaders
item.description: Nvidia has licensed intellectual property from inferencing chip designer Groq, and hired away some of its senior executives, but stopped short of an outright acquisition.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: 2026: The year we stop trusting any single cloud
item.description: For more than a decade, many considered cloud outages a theoretical risk, something to address on a whiteboard and then quietly deprioritize during cost cuts. In 2025, this risk became real.
item.dc:creator: Info World
